message:
feat(packs): add cdn.cloudfront pack for AWS CloudFront

Implements protection for AWS CloudFront CDN operations.

Safe patterns (12):
- list-distributions, list-cache-policies, list-origin-request-policies
- list-functions, list-invalidations
- get-distribution, get-distribution-config, get-cache-policy
- get-origin-request-policy, get-function, get-invalidation
- describe-function

Destructive patterns (7):
- delete-distribution (distribution removal)
- delete-cache-policy (cache policy removal)
- delete-origin-request-policy
- delete-function (CloudFront function removal)
- delete-response-headers-policy
- delete-key-group (signed URL key removal)
- create-invalidation (has cost implications)

Closes git_safety_guard-foj

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-f0d","title":"E2E logging upgrades (timings, structured logs, artifacts)","description":"## Objective

Improve E2E scripts to produce **actionable, structured logs** and timing data for failures.

## Scope

- Add per-test timing and aggregate timing summary.
- Optional JSON log output (machine-readable), alongside current human-friendly logs.
- Capture stdout/stderr on failure and store as artifacts in CI.

## Acceptance criteria

- Failures show expected vs actual plus captured output.
- Verbose mode includes timings and test IDs.
- CI artifacts include log files for failed runs.

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-f3e","title":"[PACK] secrets.vault - HashiCorp Vault Pack","description":"# HashiCorp Vault Pack

## Scope
Implement pattern matching for HashiCorp Vault CLI and API operations.

## Destructive Patterns
- `vault secrets disable` - Disables secrets engine (data loss)
- `vault kv delete` - Deletes secrets
- `vault kv destroy` - Permanently destroys secret versions
- `vault kv metadata delete` - Deletes all versions
- `vault auth disable` - Disables auth method
- `vault policy delete` - Removes policies
- `vault token revoke` - Revokes tokens
- `vault lease revoke` - Revokes leases

## Safe Patterns
- `vault read`, `vault list`
- `vault kv get`, `vault kv list`
- `vault status`, `vault version`
- `vault audit list`

## Implementation Notes
- Parent category: secrets.* (git_safety_guard-a8v)
- Keywords: vault
- Consider namespace operations for enterprise

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All destructive patterns blocked
- [ ] Safe patterns not blocked
- [ ] Tests cover common Vault workflows
- [ ] Documentation includes examples","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T14:31:00.911143116-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T17:28:08.790596553-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T17:28:08.790596553-05:00","close_reason":"Completed: secrets.vault pack already present in repo (src/packs/secrets/vault.rs + tests) and registered in pack registry. Docs update delegated to mbq7.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-f3e","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ikf2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:35.083179678-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-f3e","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-i79","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:35.124770231-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-f3e","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ct5s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:35.167848674-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-f3e","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-jdb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:35.208877791-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-f3e","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-e7j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:35.24822327-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-f3e","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-l968","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:35.288944838-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-f3e","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-qxc7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:10.129072418-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-f3e","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-6ozg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:10.172995575-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-f4f","title":"Implement ast-grep-core pattern matching layer","description":"# ast-grep-core Pattern Matching Layer

## Objective

Integrate ast-grep-core as a Rust library for structural pattern matching in heredoc/inline-script content.

## Architecture decision

Prefer **embedded ast-grep-core** over shelling out to a CLI, because:

- avoids per-invocation process spawn overhead
- keeps a single-binary distribution story
- enables richer match metadata (spans) for `dcg explain`

(Still subject to ADR confirmation; this task implements whichever embedded approach is chosen.)

## Dependencies to add (tentative)

```toml
[dependencies]
ast-grep-core = "0.40"
ast-grep-language = { version = "0.37", features = [
    "bash",
    "python",
    "javascript",
    "typescript",
    "ruby"
    # Note: Perl may not be available
] }
```

## API design

```rust
/// Pattern matcher using ast-grep-core
pub struct AstMatcher {
    patterns: HashMap<Language, Vec<CompiledPattern>>,
}

impl AstMatcher {
    /// Create matcher with default destructive patterns
    pub fn new() -> Self;

    /// Load patterns from config / registry
    pub fn from_config(config: &PatternConfig) -> Result<Self>;

    /// Match patterns against code (bounded)
    pub fn find_matches(&self, code: &str, language: Language) -> Result<Vec<PatternMatch>, MatchError>;
}

/// Result of pattern matching
pub struct PatternMatch {
    pub rule_id: String,             // stable: pack_id:pattern_name
    pub reason: String,
    pub matched_text_preview: String,
    pub start: usize,                // byte offset (or row/col)
    pub end: usize,
    pub line_number: usize,
    pub severity: Severity,          // ties into decision policy
}

pub enum Severity { Error, Warning, Info }
```

## Pattern definition format (example)

Patterns should be defined and reviewed under `git_safety_guard-6sg` (pattern library structure). The matching layer must be agnostic to *what* the patterns are, but must support:

- stable IDs
- per-pattern severity
- per-pattern suggestions (optional)

## Error handling / fail-open

In hook mode:

- Unsupported language → skip analysis (allow) + trace marker
- Parse error → skip analysis (allow) + trace marker
- Timeout → skip analysis (allow) + trace marker

In `dcg test` / `dcg explain` / `dcg scan`:

- return structured diagnostics (not silent skip)

## Performance targets (initial)

- Pattern compilation: one-time at startup (bounded)
- Parse: <2ms for typical heredoc sizes
- Match: <1ms typical
- Total AST path: bounded by a hard timeout

## Acceptance Criteria

- Implements an embedded AST matcher that can:
  - parse at least one supported language
  - match at least one simple pattern
  - return match spans + line number
- Enforces a hard timeout for AST work and fails open in hook mode on timeout.
- Produces match metadata compatible with:
  - `dcg explain` highlighting (matched span + preview)
  - rule attribution (stable rule_id)
  - decision policy (severity)
- Includes unit tests for:
  - a positive match
  - a negative (safe) snippet
  - an unsupported-language path
  - a timeout path (if testable)
","notes":"## Review Additions (2026-01-08)

### False positive control
This layer must support the FP-immunity strategy, not undermine it:

- matchers should attach stable rule IDs + severity so policy can default-warn vs deny
- matchers should support negative fixtures / “must-not-match” tests per rule family
- prefer composite/high-signal rules (AST match + literal destructive payload) over broad matches (e.g., `exec($VAR)`)

### Tests
- For each supported language: at least one positive and one negative snippet.
- Span/line mapping test: matches return accurate location for explain output.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T18:32:24.313325332-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-08T02:41:21.25882879-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-08T02:41:21.25882879-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented ast-grep-core pattern matching layer with AstMatcher struct, find_matches(), timeout handling, and 17 unit tests. All acceptance criteria met.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-f4f","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-5ib","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T18:32:32.909572665-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-f4f","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-du4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T18:32:32.929896409-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-f4f","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-yza","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:50:18.965756128-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"git_safety_guard-foj","title":"[PACK] cdn.cloudfront - AWS CloudFront Pack","description":"# AWS CloudFront CDN Pack

## Scope
Implement pattern matching for AWS CloudFront operations.

## Destructive Patterns
### aws CLI
- `aws cloudfront delete-distribution`
- `aws cloudfront delete-cache-policy`
- `aws cloudfront delete-origin-request-policy`
- `aws cloudfront delete-function`
- `aws cloudfront create-invalidation` (costly, can affect caching)

### Origin Changes
- `aws cloudfront update-distribution` with origin removal

## Safe Patterns
- `aws cloudfront list-distributions`
- `aws cloudfront get-distribution`
- `aws cloudfront get-distribution-config`
- `aws cloudfront list-invalidations`

## Implementation Notes
- Parent category: cdn.* (git_safety_guard-wso)
- Keywords: aws, cloudfront
- Note: Invalidations have cost implications

## Testing Requirements
- [ ] Unit tests for distribution deletion
- [ ] Unit tests for policy deletion
- [ ] E2E test: `scripts/e2e_tests/cdn_cloudfront.txt`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Distribution deletion blocked
- [ ] Policy deletion blocked
- [ ] List/get operations allowed","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:11:16.954912451-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-09T15:11:16.954912451-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-foj","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ikf2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:43.264189343-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-foj","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-i79","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:43.30989559-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-foj","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ct5s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:43.355790301-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-foj","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-jdb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:43.402243183-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-foj","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-e7j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:43.447889247-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-foj","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-l968","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:43.495160869-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-foj","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-qxc7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:13.088050472-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-foj","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-6ozg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:13.133330634-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-fpim","title":"Implement false positive reduction for string arguments","description":"# False Positive Reduction Implementation

## Problem

Current regex patterns match dangerous strings even in safe contexts:
- `bd create --description="This blocks rm -rf"` → BLOCKED (wrong!)
- `git commit -m "Fix rm -rf pattern"` → BLOCKED (wrong!)
- `grep "rm -rf" patterns.txt` → BLOCKED (wrong!)

## Solution

Parse command structure to identify execution context before applying destructive patterns.

## Implementation

### 1. Safe Command Registry

```rust
static SAFE_STRING_ARG_COMMANDS: &[(&str, &[&str])] = &[
    ("bd", &["--description", "--title", "-d", "-t"]),
    ("git", &["commit -m", "tag -m", "notes add -m"]),
    ("grep", &["-e", "--regexp"]),
    ("rg", &["-e", "--regexp"]),
    ("echo", &[]),  // all args are strings
    ("printf", &[]),
];
```

### 2. Execution Context Detection

```rust
enum ExecutionContext {
    Direct,           // Command itself is executed
    StringArgument,   // Inside quoted string argument to safe command
    HeredocBody,      // Inside heredoc (needs Tier 2 analysis)
    PipeTarget,       // Right side of pipe (executed)
    CommandSubst,     // Inside $() or backticks (executed)
}

fn detect_execution_context(cmd: &str, position: usize) -> ExecutionContext;
```

### 3. Integration Point

Modify main check flow:

```rust
fn check_command(cmd: &str) -> CheckResult {
    // Quick reject (existing)
    if global_quick_reject(cmd) {
        return CheckResult::allowed();
    }

    // NEW: Check if dangerous patterns are in safe context
    if is_safe_string_context(cmd) {
        return CheckResult::allowed();
    }

    // Continue with heredoc detection and pattern matching...
}
```

## Test Cases

### Must ALLOW (false positive prevention)
- `bd create --description="Pattern blocks rm -rf commands"`
- `git commit -m "Fix git reset --hard detection"`
- `grep "rm -rf" src/patterns.rs`
- `echo "example: git push --force"`
- `rg -e "DROP TABLE" --type sql`

### Must BLOCK (true positives preserved)
- `rm -rf /tmp/*`
- `git reset --hard HEAD~5`
- `bash -c "rm -rf /"`
- `echo "hi" | bash -c "rm -rf /"`

## Performance

- < 100 microseconds additional overhead
- Simple string prefix matching for command detection
- No regex needed for this layer

## Dependencies

- Depends on: False positive design (2cu)

## Acceptance Criteria

- The documented false-positive examples are allowed in hook mode and `dcg test`.
- True-positive executions are still blocked, including destructive strings inside:
  - `bash -c ...`
  - `sh -c ...`
  - `node -e ...` (where the code executes)
  - command substitution (`$(...)` / backticks)
- Context classification is conservative:
  - only skips matching inside explicitly-registered safe string arguments
  - does not treat unknown flags/commands as safe
- Performance overhead is bounded (target < 100µs additional overhead on typical allow-path commands).
","notes":"## Objective

Implement the execution-context + safe-string-arg behavior described in `git_safety_guard-t8x` so dcg stops blocking documentation/testing workflows (commit messages, bd descriptions, grep/rg patterns) while still blocking real executions.

## Review Additions (2026-01-08)

(Kept here so the task remains self-contained even though the description starts with Problem/Solution.)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PurpleRobin","created_at":"2026-01-07T20:00:00-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-08T01:52:22.183030324-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-08T01:52:22.183030324-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented safe-string-arg sanitizer + integrated into hook/evaluator; added unit/E2E regression coverage","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-fpim","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-2cu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T19:28:32.226122941-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-fpim","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-t8x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T20:02:22.808621071-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-fpim","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-t8x.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T20:03:42.899745326-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-fpim","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-t8x.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T20:03:47.932828938-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-fpim","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-99e.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T20:03:52.966413813-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-fu8","title":"Implement docker-compose extractor","description":"## Objective

Implement an extractor for docker-compose files to detect executable command contexts.

## File Pattern

- `docker-compose.yml`
- `docker-compose.yaml`
- `compose.yml`
- `compose.yaml`

## Extraction Targets

### Command Blocks (executed)
- `command:` values
- `entrypoint:` values
- `healthcheck.test:` commands

### NOT Extracted (data only)
- `environment:` values
- `labels:` values
- Volume paths
- Comments

## Example

```yaml
services:
  app:
    command: /bin/sh -c "rm -rf /data/*"  # EXTRACT THIS
    environment:
      CLEANUP: "rm -rf /"  # DO NOT extract
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- Correctly extracts command/entrypoint with line numbers
- Handles both string and array formats
- Ignores environment variables and labels
- Unit tests with representative fixtures","notes":"## Review Additions (2026-01-08)

### False positive controls
- Extract only from executed compose fields (e.g., `command`, `entrypoint`) and avoid scanning:
  - environment variables
  - comments
  - labels/metadata
- Include negative fixtures for dangerous strings in env/labels.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:24:57.298774964-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-09T13:11:56.093978239-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-09T13:11:56.093978239-05:00","close_reason":"Completed: docker-compose extractor with 9 tests. Extracts command/entrypoint/healthcheck.test, correctly ignores environment/labels/volumes/ports. 701 tests pass.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-fu8","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:25:20.580453194-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-fu8","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:46:02.44142759-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-fuzz","title":"Implement fuzzing and property-based testing for heredoc parser","description":"# Fuzzing and Property-Based Testing

## Objective

Ensure heredoc detection is robust against malformed, malicious, or unexpected input through automated testing.

## Fuzzing with cargo-fuzz

### Setup

```toml
# Cargo.toml
[dev-dependencies]
libfuzzer-sys = "0.4"
arbitrary = { version = "1", features = ["derive"] }
```

### Fuzz Targets

```rust
// fuzz/fuzz_targets/heredoc_extraction.rs
#![no_main]
use libfuzzer_sys::fuzz_target;

fuzz_target!(|data: &str| {
    // Should never panic
    let _ = extract_heredoc(data);
});

// fuzz/fuzz_targets/language_detection.rs
fuzz_target!(|data: &str| {
    let _ = detect_language(data);
});

// fuzz/fuzz_targets/tier1_regex.rs
fuzz_target!(|data: &str| {
    let _ = might_contain_heredoc(data);
});

// fuzz/fuzz_targets/full_pipeline.rs
fuzz_target!(|data: &str| {
    // Full check should never panic
    let _ = check_command(data);
});
```

### Running fuzzing

```bash
cargo install cargo-fuzz
cargo +nightly fuzz run heredoc_extraction -- -max_len=100000
cargo +nightly fuzz run full_pipeline -- -max_total_time=3600
```

## Property-Based Testing with proptest

```toml
[dev-dependencies]
proptest = "1.4"
```

## Structured fuzzing

Use `arbitrary` to generate structured heredoc inputs with variants (standard/indented/quoted).

## CI Integration

Run a bounded fuzz budget on a schedule (weekly) and allow manual trigger.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Fuzz targets exist for at least:
  - tier1 trigger
  - heredoc extraction
  - language detection
  - full pipeline
- Fuzzing runs for a bounded time in CI (scheduled) and produces reproducible crash artifacts.
- Property tests encode key invariants:
  - no panics
  - tier1 trigger is a superset of tier2 extraction triggers (no false negatives)
  - determinism (same input → same decision) where applicable
- Any discovered crash is converted into a minimal repro regression test.

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-re7","title":"[PACK] remote.scp - SCP Pack","description":"# SCP Remote Copy Pack

## Scope
Implement pattern matching for SCP operations.

## Destructive Patterns
### Overwrite Operations
- Copying to critical system paths
- Overwriting without backup
- Wrong direction copy (source/dest swapped)

### Recursive Operations
- `scp -r` to sensitive directories

## Safe Patterns
- `scp file user@host:path` (standard copy)
- `scp user@host:file .` (download)
- Copying to user directories

## Implementation Notes
- Parent category: remote.* (git_safety_guard-g5o)
- Keywords: scp
- Note: scp is being deprecated in favor of sftp/rsync

## Testing Requirements
- [ ] Unit tests for critical path overwrites
- [ ] Unit tests for recursive operations
- [ ] E2E test: `scripts/e2e_tests/remote_scp.txt`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Critical path overwrites detected
- [ ] Standard file copies allowed","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:11:14.428556049-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T03:08:12.41317512-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-11T03:08:12.41317512-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented remote.scp pack with 8 destructive patterns and 5 safe patterns; all 9 tests pass","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-re7","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ikf2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:43.859292589-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-re7","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-i79","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:43.904909447-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-re7","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ct5s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:43.950848202-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-re7","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-jdb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:43.998184937-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-re7","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-e7j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:44.044047828-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-re7","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-l968","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:44.089802537-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-re7","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-qxc7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:13.272188302-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-re7","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-6ozg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:13.318489576-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-rkg9","title":"[PACK] storage.minio - MinIO Pack","description":"# MinIO Object Storage Pack

## Scope
Implement pattern matching for MinIO (S3-compatible) operations.

## Destructive Patterns
### mc (MinIO Client)
- `mc rb` - Remove bucket
- `mc rb --force` - Force remove
- Remove with recursive flag
- `mc admin bucket delete`

## Safe Patterns
- `mc ls`
- `mc cat`
- `mc stat`
- `mc cp` (copy)
- `mc diff`
- `mc admin info`

## Implementation Notes
- Parent category: storage.* (git_safety_guard-skt)
- Keywords: mc (MinIO client)
- Note: S3-compatible, similar patterns to aws s3

## Testing Requirements
- [ ] Unit tests for bucket removal
- [ ] Unit tests for recursive deletion
- [ ] E2E test: `scripts/e2e_tests/storage_minio.txt`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Bucket removal blocked
- [ ] Recursive deletion blocked
- [ ] List/copy operations allowed","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:12:40.626641176-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-09T15:12:40.626641176-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-rkg9","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ikf2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:39.487828632-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-rkg9","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-i79","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:39.530312907-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-rkg9","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ct5s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:39.573780863-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-rkg9","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-jdb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:39.6171008-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-rkg9","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-e7j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:39.662252443-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-rkg9","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-l968","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:39.707883469-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-rkg9","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-qxc7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:11.739543506-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-rkg9","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-6ozg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:11.787195064-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-rl8","title":"Enforce coverage thresholds in CI (llvm-cov)","description":"## Objective

Turn coverage reporting from informational to **enforced**, with documented thresholds and failure behavior.

## Scope

- Use `cargo llvm-cov` output already produced in CI.
- Define initial minimum thresholds (global + key modules).
- Fail CI when thresholds are not met.

## Notes

- Thresholds should be set **after** baseline audit (see dependencies).
- Keep thresholds modest at first, with a follow-up plan to ratchet.

## Acceptance criteria

- CI fails when coverage drops below defined thresholds.
- Coverage summary still attached as artifact / job summary.
- Thresholds documented in the repo (doc or CI comments).

-{"id":"git_safety_guard-rm2n","title":"[TASK] User experience for pack discovery and feedback","description":"# User Experience for Pack Discovery and Feedback

## Scope
Ensure users can easily discover, configure, and provide feedback on packs.

## Deliverables

### 1. Pack Discovery CLI Commands
Implement new dcg commands:
```bash
# List all available packs
dcg packs list

# Show details about a specific pack
dcg packs info secrets.vault

# Show which packs would match a command
dcg explain 'vault secrets disable foo'

# Show packs enabled in current config
dcg packs enabled
```

### 2. Pack Configuration UX
Make configuration intuitive:
```toml
# .dcg.toml example with clear documentation
[packs]
# Enable all Tier 1 packs (critical security)
tier1 = true

# Selectively enable Tier 2 packs
enable = ["dns.cloudflare", "monitoring.datadog"]

# Disable specific packs if needed  
disable = ["backup.rclone"]  # We use custom backup scripts
```

### 3. Helpful Error Messages
When a command is blocked:
```
⚠️  BLOCKED: vault secrets disable production-secrets

Pack: secrets.vault
Reason: 'vault secrets disable' permanently disables a secrets engine,
        potentially causing data loss for all secrets stored there.

This looks intentional? You can:
  1. Add to allowlist: dcg allow 'vault secrets disable production-secrets'
  2. Run with bypass: DCG_BYPASS=1 vault secrets disable production-secrets
  3. Report false positive: https://github.com/.../issues/new?template=false_positive

Docs: https://dcg.dev/packs/secrets/vault
```

### 4. Feedback Collection
- GitHub issue templates for:
  - False positive reports
  - False negative reports  
  - New pack requests
- Telemetry (opt-in) for pattern match statistics
- User survey mechanism

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Pack list/info commands implemented
- [ ] Configuration is well-documented
- [ ] Error messages are helpful and actionable
- [ ] Feedback channels are clear and accessible","notes":"Testing/Validation:
- Unit tests for CLI output shape, sorting, and redaction.
- E2E CLI scenarios (list/detail/configure/feedback) using the shared harness with verbose logs.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:35:00.15029369-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-09T17:03:12.642746777-05:00"}

- Domain authentication removal

- Webhook deletion
- Event webhook disabling

- `GET /v3/templates`
- `GET /v3/api_keys`
- Template listing
- Stats retrieval
- Email sending (POST, not destructive to config)

- Parent category: email.* (git_safety_guard-62v)
- Keywords: sendgrid
- Note: API-driven, no official CLI

- [ ] Unit tests for template deletion
- [ ] Unit tests for API key deletion
- [ ] Unit tests for domain removal
- [ ] E2E test: `scripts/e2e_tests/email_sendgrid.txt`

- [ ] Template deletion blocked
- [ ] API key deletion blocked
- [ ] Domain removal blocked
- [ ] Sending/query operations allowed","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:12:04.483108048-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-09T15:12:04.483108048-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-rwu","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ikf2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:41.083500513-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-rwu","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-i79","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:41.127704464-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-rwu","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ct5s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:41.171107367-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-rwu","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-jdb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:41.216548545-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-rwu","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-e7j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:41.258989168-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-rwu","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-l968","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:41.303102468-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-rwu","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-qxc7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:12.309864117-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-rwu","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-6ozg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:12.356907019-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

- Validate precedence: project > user > system.
- Validate conditions/env gating and expiration handling across layers.

## Logging

- Print the layer that matched (when verbose) and reason in failure output.

## Acceptance criteria

- E2E demonstrates correct allowlist precedence and condition handling.
- Tests are hermetic (no reliance on real user allowlists).

@@ -0,0 +1,214 @@
+//! AWS CloudFront pack - protections for destructive CloudFront CLI operations.
+//!
+//! Covers destructive operations:
+//! - Distribution deletion (`aws cloudfront delete-distribution`)
+//! - Cache policy deletion (`aws cloudfront delete-cache-policy`)
+//! - Origin request policy deletion (`aws cloudfront delete-origin-request-policy`)
+//! - Function deletion (`aws cloudfront delete-function`)
+//! - Cache invalidation (costly, can affect caching)
+
+use crate::packs::{DestructivePattern, Pack, SafePattern};
+use crate::{destructive_pattern, safe_pattern};
+
+/// Create the AWS CloudFront pack.
+#[must_use]
+pub fn create_pack() -> Pack {
+    Pack {
+        id: "cdn.cloudfront".to_string(),
+        name: "AWS CloudFront",
+        description: "Protects against destructive AWS CloudFront operations like deleting \
+                      distributions, cache policies, and functions.",
+        keywords: &["cloudfront"],
+        safe_patterns: create_safe_patterns(),
+        destructive_patterns: create_destructive_patterns(),
+        keyword_matcher: None,
+    }
+}
+
+fn create_safe_patterns() -> Vec<SafePattern> {
+    vec![
+        // List operations
+        safe_pattern!(
+            "cloudfront-list-distributions",
+            r"aws\s+cloudfront\s+list-distributions\b"
+        ),
+        safe_pattern!(
+            "cloudfront-list-cache-policies",
+            r"aws\s+cloudfront\s+list-cache-policies\b"
+        ),
+        safe_pattern!(
+            "cloudfront-list-origin-request-policies",
+            r"aws\s+cloudfront\s+list-origin-request-policies\b"
+        ),
+        safe_pattern!(
+            "cloudfront-list-functions",
+            r"aws\s+cloudfront\s+list-functions\b"
+        ),
+        safe_pattern!(
+            "cloudfront-list-invalidations",
+            r"aws\s+cloudfront\s+list-invalidations\b"
+        ),
+        // Get operations
+        safe_pattern!(
+            "cloudfront-get-distribution",
+            r"aws\s+cloudfront\s+get-distribution\b"
+        ),
+        safe_pattern!(
+            "cloudfront-get-distribution-config",
+            r"aws\s+cloudfront\s+get-distribution-config\b"
+        ),
+        safe_pattern!(
+            "cloudfront-get-cache-policy",
+            r"aws\s+cloudfront\s+get-cache-policy\b"
+        ),
+        safe_pattern!(
+            "cloudfront-get-origin-request-policy",
+            r"aws\s+cloudfront\s+get-origin-request-policy\b"
+        ),
+        safe_pattern!(
+            "cloudfront-get-function",
+            r"aws\s+cloudfront\s+get-function\b"
+        ),
+        safe_pattern!(
+            "cloudfront-get-invalidation",
+            r"aws\s+cloudfront\s+get-invalidation\b"
+        ),
+        // Describe operations
+        safe_pattern!(
+            "cloudfront-describe-function",
+            r"aws\s+cloudfront\s+describe-function\b"
+        ),
+    ]
+}
+
+fn create_destructive_patterns() -> Vec<DestructivePattern> {
+    vec![
+        // Distribution deletion
+        destructive_pattern!(
+            "cloudfront-delete-distribution",
+            r"aws\s+cloudfront\s+delete-distribution\b",
+            "aws cloudfront delete-distribution removes a CloudFront distribution."
+        ),
+        // Cache policy deletion
+        destructive_pattern!(
+            "cloudfront-delete-cache-policy",
+            r"aws\s+cloudfront\s+delete-cache-policy\b",
+            "aws cloudfront delete-cache-policy removes a cache policy."
+        ),
+        // Origin request policy deletion
+        destructive_pattern!(
+            "cloudfront-delete-origin-request-policy",
+            r"aws\s+cloudfront\s+delete-origin-request-policy\b",
+            "aws cloudfront delete-origin-request-policy removes an origin request policy."
+        ),
+        // Function deletion
+        destructive_pattern!(
+            "cloudfront-delete-function",
+            r"aws\s+cloudfront\s+delete-function\b",
+            "aws cloudfront delete-function removes a CloudFront function."
+        ),
+        // Response headers policy deletion
+        destructive_pattern!(
+            "cloudfront-delete-response-headers-policy",
+            r"aws\s+cloudfront\s+delete-response-headers-policy\b",
+            "aws cloudfront delete-response-headers-policy removes a response headers policy."
+        ),
+        // Key group deletion
+        destructive_pattern!(
+            "cloudfront-delete-key-group",
+            r"aws\s+cloudfront\s+delete-key-group\b",
+            "aws cloudfront delete-key-group removes a key group used for signed URLs."
+        ),
+        // Invalidation (has cost implications)
+        destructive_pattern!(
+            "cloudfront-create-invalidation",
+            r"aws\s+cloudfront\s+create-invalidation\b",
+            "aws cloudfront create-invalidation creates a cache invalidation (has cost implications)."
+        ),
+    ]
+}
+
+#[cfg(test)]
+mod tests {
+    use super::*;
+    use crate::packs::test_helpers::*;
+
+    #[test]
+    fn test_pack_creation() {
+        let pack = create_pack();
+        assert_eq!(pack.id, "cdn.cloudfront");
+        assert_eq!(pack.name, "AWS CloudFront");
+        assert!(!pack.description.is_empty());
+        assert!(pack.keywords.contains(&"cloudfront"));
+
+        assert_patterns_compile(&pack);
+        assert_all_patterns_have_reasons(&pack);
+        assert_unique_pattern_names(&pack);
+    }
+
+    #[test]
+    fn allows_safe_commands() {
+        let pack = create_pack();
+        // List operations
+        assert_safe_pattern_matches(&pack, "aws cloudfront list-distributions");
+        assert_safe_pattern_matches(&pack, "aws cloudfront list-cache-policies");
+        assert_safe_pattern_matches(&pack, "aws cloudfront list-origin-request-policies");
+        assert_safe_pattern_matches(&pack, "aws cloudfront list-functions");
+        assert_safe_pattern_matches(&pack, "aws cloudfront list-invalidations --distribution-id ABC");
+        // Get operations
+        assert_safe_pattern_matches(&pack, "aws cloudfront get-distribution --id ABC");
+        assert_safe_pattern_matches(&pack, "aws cloudfront get-distribution-config --id ABC");
+        assert_safe_pattern_matches(&pack, "aws cloudfront get-cache-policy --id XYZ");
+        assert_safe_pattern_matches(&pack, "aws cloudfront get-origin-request-policy --id XYZ");
+        assert_safe_pattern_matches(&pack, "aws cloudfront get-function --name myfunc");
+        assert_safe_pattern_matches(&pack, "aws cloudfront get-invalidation --distribution-id ABC --id INV");
+        assert_safe_pattern_matches(&pack, "aws cloudfront describe-function --name myfunc");
+    }
+
+    #[test]
+    fn blocks_destructive_commands() {
+        let pack = create_pack();
+        // Distribution deletion
+        assert_blocks_with_pattern(
+            &pack,
+            "aws cloudfront delete-distribution --id ABC --if-match ETAG",
+            "cloudfront-delete-distribution",
+        );
+        // Cache policy deletion
+        assert_blocks_with_pattern(
+            &pack,
+            "aws cloudfront delete-cache-policy --id XYZ",
+            "cloudfront-delete-cache-policy",
+        );
+        // Origin request policy deletion
+        assert_blocks_with_pattern(
+            &pack,
+            "aws cloudfront delete-origin-request-policy --id XYZ",
+            "cloudfront-delete-origin-request-policy",
+        );
+        // Function deletion
+        assert_blocks_with_pattern(
+            &pack,
+            "aws cloudfront delete-function --name myfunc --if-match ETAG",
+            "cloudfront-delete-function",
+        );
+        // Response headers policy deletion
+        assert_blocks_with_pattern(
+            &pack,
+            "aws cloudfront delete-response-headers-policy --id ABC",
+            "cloudfront-delete-response-headers-policy",
+        );
+        // Key group deletion
+        assert_blocks_with_pattern(
+            &pack,
+            "aws cloudfront delete-key-group --id ABC",
+            "cloudfront-delete-key-group",
+        );
+        // Invalidation (costly)
+        assert_blocks_with_pattern(
+            &pack,
+            "aws cloudfront create-invalidation --distribution-id ABC --paths '/*'",
+            "cloudfront-create-invalidation",
+        );
+    }
+}
